entries.name: Aldwin — Count Edmund's sister-son & now the last of his blood; the boy-regent Shake saved & made; holds besieged Hollowford on Shake's promise of relief; the next target of the line-killers

Count Halfdan — deep-north-backed northern marcher lord (Skarrgaard / Cloven Strand); a relief target whose horse could make the muster an army; moves for his own gain (the North Coast succession); a marriage to Eadgyth is the coin he'd want — and Shake withheld it (a rider's now out to him)
Wulf — Count Edmund's doomsman/reeve, six-fingered; never a flat 'no'; ally left holding the keep's law in besieged Hollowford; GM-secret: divided loyalties (the law/Crown above Edmund)
The Crown delegation (the Regency) — a relief sponsor to court (would relieve Hollowford to deny it to Ranulf); envoy Aldhelm already has Shake's name in a ledger; a rider's now out to them

The Stonebay marauder-captain — CAPTURED. Cray sold him Shake's road & nature; he sprung the copse ambush (killed Oswin, shouted Shake's drift-marked secret) but was ridden down & taken alive — and ABANDONED by the web that spent him (bitter, maybe ready to talk). Shake's 2nd live web-captive & best lever yet

The Ash-Captain — low-born foreign (Ostmark) warlord besieging Hollowford (~2,500-3,000); risen from nothing, secretly craving legitimacy; his host coordinated with the line-killers — and now the Creeping Sickness may be gutting his camp

Eadgyth — Count Edmund's daughter; her bitterness toward Shake has turned to ALLIANCE: at the war-council (where Shake gave her a seat) she offered him 'anything I have' to find who killed Aldred & Wystan & gutted her line. A target of the web, now Shake's committed partner in the hunt — and the last free voice of Edmund's blood

Sir Godric — young idealist knight (10 lances), sworn to Shake's cause & near-worshipful; took an assassin's blade meant for Eadgyth & nearly died — GRAVELY WOUNDED, out of action ~weeks; Shake's truest man & unwitting conscience (still innocent of Shake's design on Marlford)
Sir Brunwulf — weathered older Greenways knight who sought Shake out; brought lances + ~20 mounted archers + 2 more halls' word; the muster's solid backbone
Brother Eadwig — old infirmarer-scholar of Edenmoor, saved by Shake & at his side; learning, medicine (tending Godric now), & a Codex churchman's blessing (counters the 'drift-tainted' slur)

Thane Beorhtric of Stanmere — the March's senior hold-rank thane (~40 horse + 300 fyrd), the relief's would-be backbone; sent only a token reply, then VANISHED from his own keep 3 nights ago as a poisoned 'relief-grain' shipment loosed the plague on Stanmere (taken? hunting the poisoner? betrayed by an inside hand?). His levy is melting into fever; finding him / saving Stanmere is now a relief objective AND a web-trail

Saewulf — grizzled retired free-company captain, 'last survivor'; Shake's sworn right-hand & veteran lieutenant; holds Marlford's keep & now hunts how the assassin breached the camp (a soldier's mind, hard, his own man)

Coll Cray (FLED) — the fled profiteer turned the web's broker against Shake: seeded the tainted grain in Marlford, and SOLD Shake's road AND his drift-marked secret to the marauder-captain/the web (→ Oswin's death, the secret out). Vengeful, diminished, deep in the web's pocket — catching him cracks the breach, the buyer, and the trail

The captured assassin — a 'weighed-and-paid' line-killer (web/Bone-Sayer) sent for Eadgyth; TAKEN ALIVE & intact, his suicide-out denied (the first of his kind held breathing); fanatically silent so far, but a live interrogation lead to the head/paymaster
